items.question: tsc vs a bundler like Vite — what does each do?
items.answer: tsc is the TypeScript compiler: it type-checks and can emit JS. A bundler (Vite/webpack) builds the module graph and produces optimized, packaged output (tree-shaken, minified, split), often using a fast transpiler that skips type-checking. Many setups use both.

items.question: Why do builds get smaller with tree-shaking?
items.answer: Because the bundler statically analyzes ESM imports and removes exports that are never used, so dead code isn't shipped to the browser.

items.question: What breaks if you trust esbuild/Vite to catch type errors?
items.answer: Type errors reach production, because those fast transpilers strip types without checking them. You need a separate tsc step to catch them.
